title: lithocarpus solerianus
intro: Lithocarpus solerianus, also known as the Soler's copper beech, is a species of evergreen tree native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Lithocarpus solerianus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and aid in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the tree are often dried and made into tea or infusions for medicinal use.
